info.title: Omaya backend
info.description:
Portal backend for the Omaya maternal-health AI voice-call platform. Schedules calls, ingests completed-call records, and never speaks to Twilio or xAI directly (that's the call-service's job).

**Auth (Phase 3).** All clinical endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`. Tokens are issued by `POST /auth/sign-in` (HS256, 12h expiry). The JWT carries `hospital_id` + `role` + `must_change_password`; the auth middleware extracts these into `request.state` and feeds them to `tenant_session()` so every clinical query runs RLS-scoped to the caller's hospital. The dev-only `X-Test-Hospital-Id` header from Phase 2 has been removed in every environment.

**Multi-tenancy (Phase 2).** Postgres RLS is enabled + forced on `mothers`, `clinicians`, `scheduled_calls`, `calls`, plus the Phase 4 tables `alerts`, `sms_log`, `audit_phi_events`. Composite FKs on `(id, hospital_id)` reject cross-tenant references at the DB layer — see `tests/test_tenancy_poison.py` for the regression suite.

**SMS pipeline + L4 escalation (Phase 4).** Outbound SMS is sent by the `sms-worker` Railway service via the `SmsProvider` abstraction in `services/sms/`. The `escalation-cron` service advances open L4 alerts every minute; Twilio status callbacks land on `POST /webhooks/sms/status` and trigger the next escalation step immediately on terminal-failure receipts. L3 alerts are dashboard-only — no SMS — to keep the L4 channel trusted. Two per-mother rate caps (5/24h mother-bound, 10/24h clinician-per-mother) protect against runaway send patterns.

**Reference page:** `/reference` renders this OpenAPI document via Scalar; `/docs` and `/redoc` are FastAPI's defaults and stay mounted for compatibility.

get.description: Returns `{"status": "ok"}` whenever the FastAPI process is serving traffic. Used by Railway's healthcheck.

post.description:
Service-to-service post-call ingest. Called by `post_call_worker` once the call-service has uploaded audio + transcript to Supabase Storage and classified severity.

**Auth.** `X-Omaya-Internal-Token` (constant-time compared against `INGEST_SHARED_SECRET`). **No** JWT — this is service-to-service.

**Idempotency.** Dedup on `body.call_id` under the request's `tenant_session(body.hospital_id)`. Replays return `deduplicated=true`; cross-tenant replays (same `call_id`, different `hospital_id`) fall through to the INSERT path and are rejected by the composite FK on `calls(mother_id, hospital_id) → mothers(id, hospital_id)`.

**Phase 4 — alert generation.** After the `calls` row commits, alert rows are written based on `body.severity`:

- `L1` — note-only on the `calls` row; **no `alerts` row**.
- `L2` — `alerts` row with `status='open'`, `last_escalation_step=0`. Dashboard task only; no SMS.
- `L3` — `alerts` row with `status='open'`, `last_escalation_step=0`. Surfaces as a 24h follow-up task on the clinician dashboard; no SMS (`escalation_cron` filters `severity='L4'` at the query level).
- `L4` — `alerts` row with `status='open'`, `last_escalation_step=1`, plus **two** `sms_job` rows enqueued in the same transaction: the primary-clinician step-1 page (keyed by `(mother_id, alert_id, 1)` against `uq_sms_log_idempotency`) and the mother-facing L4 SMS (keyed by `(mother_id, alert_id, NULL)`, coexisting via NULLS DISTINCT).

Phone numbers + sender are read from `hospitals.config.escalation` via the `HospitalConfig` Pydantic validator; a malformed config raises 500 so the post-call worker retries and ops sees the validation error. Both alert + queue writes share the single `tenant_session` transaction — partial state (`alerts` row without enqueued SMS, or vice versa) is impossible.

500.description: `INGEST_SHARED_SECRET` not configured, or `hospitals.config` failed `HospitalConfig` validation on an `L4` ingest (worker retries are the recovery path).

post.description: Enqueues a `call_job` for `mother_id` (bypasses the scheduler — E2E / admin override). Ack only; subscribe to ingest for outcome.

422.description: `mother_id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

post.summary: Send consent-confirmation SMS to the mother
post.description:
Phase 4 SMS-pipeline stub. Enqueues one `sms_job` row with `message_type='consent_confirm'` and `recipient_role='mother'`. The SMS worker renders the template, calls Twilio, and writes the matching `sms_log` row.

**Auth.** JWT; `tenant_required` scopes the mother + hospital lookup to the JWT's `hospital_id`. Cross-tenant `mother_id` surfaces as 404.

**Phase 4 scope.** Only exercises the SMS pipeline path — there is intentionally **no** idempotency key on these enqueues. Phase 9 wires the consent state machine and the per-state idempotency. The interim guard against double-click spam is the mother-bound 5/24h cap (`SMS_RATE_CAP_MOTHER_PER_24H`) enforced by the SMS worker before contacting Twilio.

Returns 202 with `{"status": "enqueued", "job_id": ...}`. The job-id is the `job_queue.id` and is opaque to clients.

schema.title: Response Confirm Consent Mothers  Mother Id  Confirm Consent Post

404.description: Mother not found in caller's tenant.

500.description: Invariant violation — mother's `hospital_id` resolved to an unreachable hospital row. No PHI in the detail.

post.summary: Send withdrawal-confirmation SMS to the mother
post.description:
Phase 4 SMS-pipeline stub. Enqueues one `sms_job` row with `message_type='withdrawal_confirm'` and `recipient_role='mother'`. Same posture as `confirm-consent`: JWT-gated, RLS-scoped, no idempotency key (Phase 9 owns that), interim safety is the mother-bound 5/24h cap.

Returns 202 with `{"status": "enqueued", "job_id": ...}`.

schema.title: Response Confirm Withdrawal Mothers  Mother Id  Confirm Withdrawal Post

404.description: Mother not found in caller's tenant.

500.description: Invariant violation — mother's `hospital_id` resolved to an unreachable hospital row. No PHI in the detail.

post.description: Returns a JWT on success. Failure returns a generic `invalid_credentials` error_code regardless of which step failed (anti-enumeration). 5 failures in 15 minutes locks the account for 30 minutes.
Reachable even when `must_change_password=true` (does NOT depend on `force_change_guard`). Returns a fresh JWT with the flag cleared so the caller can drop the stale one.

400.description: `weak_password` — new password fails the complexity rule

401.description: `invalid_credentials` — current password wrong, OR missing/bad JWT

post.summary: Create a clinician seat under the calling admin's hospital
post.description: Returns a one-shot `temp_password` the admin must communicate to the new clinician via a side channel. The clinician's first sign-in is force-rotated.

post.summary: Acknowledge an open alert; stops the L4 escalation chain
post.description:
Acknowledge an open `L2` / `L3` / `L4` alert.

**Auth.** `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`. The JWT-derived `hospital_id` scopes the read via a manually-opened `tenant_session`; cross-tenant lookups surface as a plain 404 (anti-API-mapping, same posture as the auth handlers).

**Effect on the L4 chain.** Setting `status='acknowledged'` stops further SMS — the `escalation_cron` filters acknowledged alerts out of both the threshold sweep and the 60s no-callback sweep, and the webhook's terminal-failure trigger also no-ops on non-`open` alerts. L2 / L3 alerts can be acknowledged through the same endpoint (purely a dashboard status flip — they never entered the SMS chain).

**Idempotency.** Re-acknowledging an already-acknowledged alert returns 200 with the **original** `acknowledged_at` and `acknowledged_by` so dashboards displaying multi-click protection don't see the timestamp jump. Re-acks **do not** write a second `audit_phi_events` row.

**Audit.** A fresh acknowledge writes one `audit_phi_events` row with `action='acknowledge'`, `resource_type='alert'` on a separate, short-lived transaction that opens **after** the alert UPDATE commits — so the audit row only lands if the ack itself actually persisted. Audit failures log + swallow; the clinician still gets a 200.

404.description: Alert not visible to caller's tenant, or doesn't exist (deliberately indistinguishable).

409.description: Alert `status='resolved'` — terminal state, no further transitions allowed.

post.description:
Twilio Programmable Messaging status callback. **Public** — no JWT; the only auth gate is the provider signature.

**Signature validation.** Twilio computes an HMAC-SHA1 over the **public URL** Twilio called plus the sorted form params, signed with `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, and sends it in the `X-Twilio-Signature` header. The handler reconstructs the URL from `request.url`, which only reflects the public origin (`https://portal-api.omaya.dev/...`) **after** `ProxyHeadersMiddleware` rewrites Railway's `X-Forwarded-Proto` / `X-Forwarded-Host`. Without that middleware, signature checks would 403 every webhook. A lifespan check in `main.py` refuses to start the process if the middleware is unmounted.

**Status mapping.** Twilio's `MessageStatus` is mapped to `sms_log.delivery_status` (`accepted`/`queued` → `queued`, `sending`/`sent` → `sent`, then `delivered`, `failed`, `undelivered` pass through). Terminal statuses (`delivered`, `failed`, `undelivered`) also set `sms_log.delivered_at = now()`.

**L4 terminal-failure trigger.** If the matching `sms_log` row is tied to an open `L4` alert AND the mapped status is `failed` or `undelivered`, the handler calls `advance_escalation` synchronously inside the same transaction — the next rung (primary → secondary → hospital_admin) is paged immediately instead of waiting up to 5 minutes for the cron sweep. The advance is race-safe vs. the cron's 60s no-callback sweep via an optimistic `UPDATE … WHERE last_escalation_step = prev` on the alert row.

**Unknown SID.** Returns 200 with `{"status": "unknown_sid"}` rather than 404 — the receipt may have beaten the worker's `sms_log` INSERT, and 4xx would trigger Twilio's retry storm instead of letting the next callback resolve the race.

**AT migration note.** The URL stays the same after the Africa's Talking migration; AT sends JSON (not form-encoded) and authenticates by IP allowlist (not HMAC). The branch lives inside `provider.validate_signature`.

200.description: Receipt accepted. `{"status": "ok"}` on a normal update; `{"status": "unknown_sid"}` when the `MessageSid` isn't in `sms_log` yet (race with the worker's INSERT — Twilio retries, next callback resolves).

schema.title: Response Sms Status Webhooks Sms Status Post

403.description: `X-Twilio-Signature` missing or HMAC mismatch. The body is **not** inspected on this path.

422.description: Form body missing `MessageSid` / `MessageStatus`, or `MessageStatus` outside Twilio's documented vocabulary.

post.summary: Onboard a new hospital + its seed hospital_admin
post.description:
Internal Omaya operator surface. Creates a hospital row and provisions the first `hospital_admin` clinician in a single transaction; returns a one-time `admin_temp_password` that ops delivers to the admin out-of-band. The admin's first sign-in is force-rotated (`must_change_password=true`).

**Auth.** `X-Omaya-Platform-Admin-Token` (constant-time compared against `PLATFORM_ADMIN_TOKEN`). **No JWT** — no admin exists pre-creation. Distinct token from `INGEST_SHARED_SECRET` so a leaked service-to-service token can't pivot to hospital creation.

**Connection.** Runs on the `omaya_platform_admin` BYPASSRLS pool because `omaya_app` (the tenant runtime role) has SELECT-only on `hospitals` (migration 0004). The endpoint is intentionally outside the tenant-scoped surface — it predates any tenant.

**Validation.** `config` carries the full `HospitalConfig` Pydantic shape — threshold bounded `[1, 60]` minutes, all four phones Ghana E.164, `sms_sender_id` Ghana E.164 or AT-style alphanumeric. Required at onboarding so a misconfigured tenant fails fast here rather than at first-L4-ingest time.

**Audit.** Writes one `audit_auth_events` row of type `password_change` for the seed admin in the same transaction. Operator-level events beyond that are logged but not yet audited to a dedicated table (deferred to Phase 11).

**One-time temp password.** The `admin_temp_password` field in the response is shown ONCE; the backend has no API to re-fetch it. Operator delivers via 1Password share / Signal / in-person handoff. NEVER log it.

409.description: `admin_email_taken` — the admin email is already used by another clinician (global UNIQUE index on `clinicians.email`).

422.description: Body validation failed — typically a malformed `HospitalConfig` (threshold out of bounds, bad E.164 phone, missing escalation block) or a typo in a field name (`extra='forbid'`).

500.description: `platform_admin_token_not_configured` — backend env var unset. Other 500s are unexpected DB errors.

status.title: Status
status.description: Always `'acknowledged'` on the success path. On a re-ack of a row already in `'acknowledged'`, the same value is returned (idempotent).

acknowledged_at.title: Acknowledged At
acknowledged_at.description: UTC timestamp the ack landed. On idempotent re-ack, this is the **original** timestamp (not `now()`) so dashboard multi-click guards don't see the value jump.

acknowledged_by.title: Acknowledged By
acknowledged_by.description: `clinicians.id` of the acknowledging clinician. On re-ack, this is the **original** acknowledger (not the re-clicker).

AcknowledgeAlertResponse.title: AcknowledgeAlertResponse
AcknowledgeAlertResponse.description:
Response from ``POST /alerts/{alert_id}/acknowledge``.

Returned on both first-acknowledge AND idempotent re-acknowledge of
an already-acked alert (the route returns 200 with the original
``acknowledged_at`` timestamp in the latter case).

ChangePasswordResponse.title: ChangePasswordResponse
ChangePasswordResponse.description:
Returns a fresh JWT with ``must_change_password=false`` so the
caller can drop the stale one on the floor.

CreateClinicianRequest.title: CreateClinicianRequest
CreateClinicianRequest.description:
``POST /admin/clinicians`` request body.

``role`` is bounded by the same CHECK constraint that's on the
``clinicians`` table — including ``hospital_admin``, which the
*handler* rejects (internal-only role; provisioned via SQL, never
via the API). We allow the literal in the schema so the 403 fires
in the handler with a precise error_code, not as a generic
validation 422.

CreateClinicianResponse.title: CreateClinicianResponse
CreateClinicianResponse.description:
201 Created — the temp_password is shown ONCE. The admin UI is
responsible for surfacing it to the new clinician via a side channel
(in-person, SMS, etc); the backend never logs it.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name of the hospital. Surfaces in clinician dashboards.

phone_number_e164.title: Phone Number E164
phone_number_e164.description: Hospital main / front-desk number, Ghana E.164. The L4 mother-facing SMS template includes this so the mother knows who to call back.

config.description: Phase 4 escalation block + ``sms_sender_id``. Validated by ``HospitalConfig`` (``extra='forbid'`` at every level; threshold bounded [1, 60] minutes; all four phone fields Ghana E.164). Required — missing or malformed config would fail the first L4 ingest, so we surface it here at 422 instead of at clinical-fire time.

admin_email.title: Admin Email
admin_email.description: Email for the seed ``hospital_admin`` clinician. Must be globally unique across the ``clinicians`` table. Lowercased + whitespace-stripped on persist.

admin_name.title: Admin Name
admin_name.description: Display name of the seed admin. Captured for audit context. The ``clinicians`` table does not yet have a ``name`` column (symmetric with ``admin_clinicians.py``); this is recorded in the audit-event row only.

CreateHospitalRequest.title: CreateHospitalRequest
CreateHospitalRequest.description:
``POST /platform/hospitals`` request body.

``extra="forbid"`` — typos like ``phone_e164`` (wrong key) fail
loudly at 422 rather than silently dropping a load-bearing field.

``config`` is REQUIRED (not Optional) — Phase 4's ingest endpoint
fails closed on L4 calls if ``hospitals.config`` is malformed; we
surface that failure at onboarding time rather than at the first
L4 page-fire. The full ``HospitalConfig`` Pydantic model validates
the escalation block, threshold bounds, and Ghana E.164 on every
phone field.

No ``id`` field on the request — the server mints ``uuid7()`` so
that hospital_id is time-sortable and not a frontend attack
surface.

hospital_id.title: Hospital Id
hospital_id.description: The newly created hospital's id. Time-sortable UUID v7.

admin_clinician_id.title: Admin Clinician Id
admin_clinician_id.description: The newly created seed admin's clinician_id.

admin_temp_password.title: Admin Temp Password
admin_temp_password.description: One-time temporary password for the seed admin. Shown ONCE in this response; the backend has no API to re-fetch it. Operator delivers to the admin via a side channel; the admin's first sign-in force-rotates.

CreateHospitalResponse.title: CreateHospitalResponse
CreateHospitalResponse.description:
201 Created — the ``admin_temp_password`` is shown ONCE.

The operator is responsible for delivering it to the new admin via
a side channel (1Password share, Signal, in-person handoff); the
backend never logs it and there is no API to re-fetch it. The
admin's first sign-in is gated on ``must_change_password=true``.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description:
All Phase 3 auth-surface errors use this shape.

``error_code`` is the stable frontend contract; ``message`` is a
human-readable tagline tuned for UI display. NEVER include
``locked_until`` (gives attackers a retry schedule), the required
role on a 403 (maps the API), or whether the email existed on a
sign-in failure (enables enumeration).

l4_acknowledgment_threshold_min.title: L4 Acknowledgment Threshold Min
l4_acknowledgment_threshold_min.description: Minutes between escalation steps. Spec default: 5 (mvp-build L1087). Floor 1 (anything tighter would melt Twilio and lose signal-to-noise); ceiling 60 (anything longer means the chain isn't really an emergency channel).

primary_oncall_phone_e164.title: Primary Oncall Phone E164
primary_oncall_phone_e164.description: Step 1 recipient — primary on-call clinician. Ghana E.164 (starts `+233`).

secondary_oncall_phone_e164.title: Secondary Oncall Phone E164
secondary_oncall_phone_e164.description: Step 2 recipient — secondary on-call clinician. Paged `l4_acknowledgment_threshold_min` after step 1 if the alert is still `status='open'`.

hospital_admin_phone_e164.title: Hospital Admin Phone E164
hospital_admin_phone_e164.description: Step 3 recipient — hospital admin. **Terminal** rung; if the admin doesn't acknowledge, the open alert stays on the dashboard but no further SMS goes out.

EscalationConfig.title: EscalationConfig
EscalationConfig.description:
The ``escalation`` block of ``hospitals.config``.

Three rungs (primary → secondary → hospital_admin) per spec L1095.
The L4 chain stops at hospital_admin — the founder is never paged
on a clinical event.

escalation.description: L4 escalation rules. The chain has exactly three rungs and stops at `hospital_admin`. L3 has no escalation config because L3 alerts never enter the SMS pipeline.

sms_sender_id.title: Sms Sender Id
sms_sender_id.description: Ghana E.164 (Twilio MVP — must be a Ghana long-code Twilio provisioned for the hospital) OR alphanumeric 3–11 chars `[A-Za-z0-9 ]` (reserved for Africa's Talking — not used in MVP).

HospitalConfig.title: HospitalConfig
HospitalConfig.description:
Full ``hospitals.config`` shape.

``extra="forbid"`` at both levels — any unknown key is a configuration
drift smell that should surface in CI / at onboarding time, not at L4
fire time.

PostCallIngest.title: PostCallIngest
PostCallIngest.description:
Wire contract from ``post_call_worker`` → backend after a call ends.

Idempotency on ``call_id``; replays return ``deduplicated=true``.
``extra="forbid"`` → unknown fields 422 instead of silent drop.

Phase 5: ``symptoms`` extends the contract. Default ``[]`` keeps
Phase 4 fixtures + an older ``post_call_worker`` deployment
backward-compatible.

SignInRequest.title: SignInRequest
SignInRequest.description:
``POST /auth/sign-in`` request body.

Note ``password`` is a plain ``str`` (not ``SecretStr``) — Pydantic's
``SecretStr`` only hides the value in ``__repr__``; the bytes still
flow through the handler, where they're fed straight to bcrypt and
discarded. The repr-hiding adds no security but does interfere with
test asserts. Phase 3 keeps it simple.

SymptomItem.title: SymptomItem
SymptomItem.description:
One symptom extracted from the transcript by Grok-4.

Phase 5: the post-call worker's classification prompt now returns a
``symptoms[]`` array alongside severity/evidence/etc. Ingest writes
one ``symptoms`` row per item in the same tx as the parent ``calls``
row.

``extra="ignore"`` (not ``forbid``) is deliberate — Grok-4 occasionally
adds drift fields like ``confidence`` or ``location`` to symptom
objects. We tolerate the drift at the item level rather than rejecting
the whole ingest payload. The outer ``PostCallIngest`` keeps
``extra="forbid"`` because the wire contract IS frozen at the call
level; only per-symptom shape varies with the LLM.

TriggerCallResponse.title: TriggerCallResponse
TriggerCallResponse.description:
Ack from ``POST /mothers/{mother_id}/calls`` — the ``call_job`` has
been enqueued; the call has not yet been placed.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Sign-in and password change. `POST /auth/sign-in` is the only public endpoint on this service — every other clinical route requires the JWT it issues. `must_change_password=true` is set for seeded + provisioned clinicians on first sign-in and gates every other endpoint with `403 password_change_required` until the clinician rotates via `POST /auth/change-password`.

tags.name: admin
tags.description: Hospital-admin provisioning surface. Restricted to `role=hospital_admin` JWTs. New clinicians come back with a one-time `temp_password` that the response shows ONCE; the admin is responsible for delivering it to the clinician out-of-band, after which the clinician's first sign-in forces a password change.

tags.name: mothers
tags.description: Clinical surface bound to the JWT-derived `hospital_id`. Every endpoint here runs inside `tenant_session(hospital_id)` so Postgres RLS gates the read AND the composite FK `mothers(id, hospital_id)` gates cross-tenant writes.

tags.name: internal
tags.description: Service-to-service ingest. Authenticated by `X-Omaya-Internal-Token` (constant-time compared against `INGEST_SHARED_SECRET`), NOT by JWT. The handler wraps the write in `tenant_session(body.hospital_id)` so misattributed payloads raise on the composite FK.

tags.name: alerts
tags.description: Clinician alert surface. `POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge` stops the L4 escalation chain. RLS-scoped via the same `tenant_required` chain as the `mothers` surface. PHI actions write to `audit_phi_events` on a separate tx (see `services/alert_audit.py`).

tags.name: webhooks
tags.description: Provider delivery-receipt webhooks. Public endpoints, authenticated by provider-specific signature validation (Twilio's `X-Twilio-Signature` HMAC in MVP; AT's IP allowlist post-migration). The L4 escalation chain uses these receipts to detect Twilio-side delivery failures and advance the next step synchronously, without waiting for the cron-based threshold sweep.

tags.name: platform
tags.description: **Internal Omaya operator surface — not exposed to hospital users or clinical staff.** Authenticated by `X-Omaya-Platform-Admin-Token` (constant-time compared against `PLATFORM_ADMIN_TOKEN`). Distinct from the `admin` tag (which scopes to a hospital_admin operating WITHIN their own tenant) and from `internal` (service-to-service ingest from the post-call-worker). Hospital onboarding lives here; future per-hospital config-update endpoints and any platform-ops debug surfaces will too. **Operationally**: the token is stored in 1Password under "Omaya / Platform admin token" and rotated on operator changes. Loss of the token does NOT compromise existing tenants — RLS still gates clinical data.
